 {"id":"bd-kbgh","title":"Implement ntm_kill_session() and ntm_interrupt_session()","description":"# Session Cleanup Functions

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Cleanup sessions after processing or on error/interrupt.

## Implementation

```bash
ntm_kill_session() {
    local session="$1"
    ntm kill "$session" -f 2>/dev/null || true
}

ntm_interrupt_session() {
    local session="$1"
    ntm --robot-interrupt="$session" 2>/dev/null || true
}
```

## Kill Session
- Force kills the tmux session
- -f flag prevents confirmation prompt
- Idempotent (safe to call on non-existent session)
- Used after workflow completes or on fatal error

## Interrupt Session
- Sends Ctrl+C to agent panes
- Used to stop long-running agent work
- Can be followed by prompt retry

## Cleanup Pattern
```bash
cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() {
    [[ "${AGENT_SWEEP_KEEP_SESSIONS:-false}" == "true" ]] && return 0
    
    local sessions
    sessions=$(ntm --robot-status 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -o '"name":"ru_sweep_[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
    for session in $sessions; do
        # Only kill sessions from this PID
        if [[ "$session" == *"_$$"* ]] || [[ "$session" == *"_$$_"* ]]; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session"
        fi
    done
}
```

## Session Preservation
Respects these flags:
- --keep-sessions: Never kill sessions
- --keep-sessions-on-fail: Keep failed repo sessions (default true)

## Trap Handler
```bash
trap 'cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions; save_agent_sweep_state "interrupted"' INT TERM
```

## Considerations
- Always capture pane output BEFORE killing session
- Capture artifacts for debugging failed repos
- Return true even on failure (cleanup should not fail sweep)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:50:13.532543977-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:05:33.945444761-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:05:33.945444761-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ntm_kill_session(), ntm_interrupt_session(), and cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() at lines 6246-6282."}

 {"id":"bd-kczb","title":"Implement cmd_agent_sweep() main function","description":"# Agent Sweep Main Command Function

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Main entry point for `ru agent-sweep` command.

## Implementation Structure

```bash
cmd_agent_sweep() {
    local with_release=false
    local parallel=1
    local repos_filter=""
    local dry_run=false
    local resume=false
    local restart=false

    # Parse arguments (while loop with case)
    # ... --with-release, -j N, --repos=, --dry-run, etc.

    # ADDED: Concurrent instance lock (prevent multiple agent-sweep runs)
    local lock_dir="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/instance.lock"
    if ! mkdir "$lock_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
        local existing_pid
        existing_pid=$(cat "$lock_dir/pid" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
        log_error "Another agent-sweep is already running (PID: $existing_pid)"
        log_error "If stale, remove: $lock_dir"
        return 1
    fi
    echo $$ > "$lock_dir/pid"
    trap 'rmdir "$lock_dir" 2>/dev/null || true' EXIT

    # Check ntm availability (fail fast)
    ntm_check_available || { log_error "..."; return 3; }

    # Check tmux
    command -v tmux &>/dev/null || { log_error "..."; return 3; }

    # Load repos from config
    local repos=()
    load_all_repos repos

    # Filter to repos with uncommitted changes
    local dirty_repos=()
    for repo_spec in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")
        if [[ -d "$repo_path" ]] && has_uncommitted_changes "$repo_path"; then
            if [[ -z "$repos_filter" ]] || [[ "$repo_spec" == *"$repos_filter"* ]]; then
                dirty_repos+=("$repo_spec")
            fi
        fi
    done

    # Handle empty case
    if [[ ${#dirty_repos[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "No repositories with uncommitted changes found."
        return 0
    fi

    # Dry run mode
    if [[ "$dry_run" == true ]]; then
        # List repos without processing
        return 0
    fi

    # Setup results tracking
    setup_agent_sweep_results

    # Handle resume/restart
    if [[ "$resume" == true ]] && load_agent_sweep_state; then
        filter_completed_repos dirty_repos
    elif [[ "$restart" == true ]]; then
        cleanup_agent_sweep_state
    fi

    # Setup trap handlers
    setup_agent_sweep_traps

    # Process (sequential or parallel)
    if [[ $parallel -gt 1 ]]; then
        run_parallel_agent_sweep dirty_repos "$parallel" "$with_release"
    else
        run_sequential_agent_sweep dirty_repos "$with_release"
    fi

    # Summary and cleanup
    print_agent_sweep_summary
    return $sweep_exit
}
```

## CLI Options to Parse
- --with-release: Enable Phase 3
- -j N, --parallel=N: Parallel workers
- --repos=PATTERN: Filter pattern
- --dry-run: Preview mode
- --resume / --restart: State recovery
- --phase{1,2,3}-timeout=N: Timeouts
- --execution-mode=MODE: plan|apply|agent
- --keep-sessions: Preserve sessions
- --keep-sessions-on-fail: Preserve failed sessions
- --attach-on-fail: Attach to first failed session for debugging
- --release-strategy=STR: Release policy
- --secret-scan=MODE: Security scanning
- --max-file-mb=N: Maximum file size limit
- --capture-lines=N: Lines to capture from pane output
- --json: JSON output mode
- --verbose / --quiet / --debug: Output control

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All CLI options parsed correctly
- [ ] Concurrent instance lock prevents multiple runs
- [ ] ntm and tmux availability checked before processing
- [ ] Repos loaded and filtered correctly
- [ ] Dry-run mode shows repos without processing
- [ ] Resume/restart state handled correctly
- [ ] Trap handlers set up for graceful shutdown
- [ ] Exit codes match ru standard (0-5)

 {"id":"bd-pfle","title":"Implement parallel preflight execution","description":"# Parallel Preflight Execution

## Parent Epic: bd-cpxq (Preflight Safety Checks)

## Purpose
Run all preflight checks upfront before starting any agents. Shows all problems at once for better UX.

## Implementation

```bash
run_parallel_preflight() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local -a passed_repos=()
    local -a failed_repos=()
    local preflight_results_file="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/preflight_results.ndjson"

    log_info "Running preflight checks on ${#repos_ref[@]} repositories..."

    # Initialize results file
    echo "{\"type\":\"header\",\"timestamp\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\"}" > "$preflight_results_file"

    # Run preflight for each repo (can be parallelized with xargs/parallel)
    for repo_spec in "${repos_ref[@]}"; do
        local repo_path
        repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")

        if repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
            passed_repos+=("$repo_spec")
            echo "{\"repo\":\"$repo_spec\",\"status\":\"passed\"}" >> "$preflight_results_file"
        else
            failed_repos+=("$repo_spec")
            echo "{\"repo\":\"$repo_spec\",\"status\":\"failed\",\"reason\":\"$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON\"}" >> "$preflight_results_file"
            log_warn "Skipping $repo_spec: $PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"
        fi
    done

    # Summary
    log_info "Preflight complete: ${#passed_repos[@]} passed, ${#failed_repos[@]} skipped"

    # Return passed repos
    repos_ref=("${passed_repos[@]}")

    # Return failure if all repos failed
    [[ ${#passed_repos[@]} -gt 0 ]]
}
```

## Integration
- Called from cmd_agent_sweep() after loading repos, before processing
- Replaces per-repo preflight in run_sequential/parallel_sweep
- Results stored for summary display

## Benefits
- Fail fast: shows all problems before starting any agents
- Better UX: user sees complete picture immediately
- Reduces wasted time: no agents spawned for repos that would fail preflight

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All repos checked before any agent starts
- [ ] Failed repos listed with reasons
- [ ] Passed repos returned for processing
- [ ] Preflight results saved for summary
- [ ] Works with --dry-run mode

 {"id":"bd-prgk","title":"Implement commit plan execution","description":"# Commit Plan Execution

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Execute validated commit plans using ru's deterministic git execution.

## Why ru Executes (Not Agent)
- Agent can ignore prompt constraints
- ru enforces security guardrails
- Deterministic, auditable execution
- Consistent with Planner→Validator→Executor model

## Implementation

```bash
# Execute commit plan after validation
# Args: $1=commit_plan_json, $2=repo_path
# Returns: 0=success, 1=failure
execute_commit_plan() {
    local plan="$1"
    local repo_path="$2"
    
    # Extract commits array
    local commits
    commits=$(json_get_field "$plan" "commits")
    
    # Parse each commit
    local commit_index=0
    while read -r commit_json; do
        ((commit_index++))
        
        local files message
        files=$(json_get_field "$commit_json" "files")
        message=$(json_get_field "$commit_json" "message")
        
        # Stage files
        for file in $files; do
            file="${file//\"/}"  # Strip quotes
            if [[ -f "$repo_path/$file" ]]; then
                git -C "$repo_path" add "$file" || {
                    log_error "Failed to stage: $file"
                    return 1
                }
            else
                log_warn "File not found, skipping: $file"
            fi
        done
        
        # Create commit
        if ! git -C "$repo_path" commit -m "$message" 2>&1; then
            log_error "Commit $commit_index failed"
            return 1
        fi
        
        log_verbose "Created commit $commit_index: ${message%%$'\n'*}"
    done <<< "$commits"
    
    # Push if requested
    local push_flag
    push_flag=$(json_get_field "$plan" "push")
    if [[ "$push_flag" == "true" ]]; then
        if ! git -C "$repo_path" push 2>&1; then
            log_error "Push failed"
            return 1
        fi
        log_verbose "Pushed to remote"
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

## Commit Message Format
Agent should produce messages with:
- Subject line (50 chars max)
- Blank line
- Body explaining why
- Co-Authored-By trailer

Example:
```
feat(auth): implement OAuth2 PKCE flow

This commit adds PKCE support for mobile clients...

🤖 Generated with Claude Code
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## Execution Mode Check
```bash
if [[ "$AGENT_SWEEP_EXECUTION_MODE" == "plan" ]]; then
    log_info "Plan mode: Saving plan without execution"
    save_plan_to_artifacts "$plan" "$artifacts_dir/commit_plan.json"
    return 0
fi
```

## Post-Execution Validation
- Verify working tree is clean: git status --porcelain
- Verify commits created: git log --oneline -N
- If push=true, verify upstream: git status (should show "up to date")

## Error Recovery
- If commit fails: Do NOT continue with remaining commits
- Preserve partial state in artifacts
- Mark repo as failed with specific error","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:52:19.841468105-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T23:09:50.943637954-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T23:09:50.943637954-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-65u3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:41.191291263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-nqjy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:41.21628008-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-0ghe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:41.235679564-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-5iwb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:41.25636029-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-8bxp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:18.116549201-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qhw3","title":"Implement real-time progress display for agent-sweep","description":"Implements progress display system for agent-sweep operations with multiple repos.

## Background

When running agent-sweep across multiple repositories, users need clear feedback about:
- Which repo is currently being processed
- What phase the agent is in
- Overall progress (X of Y repos complete)
- Estimated time remaining (optional)
- Any warnings or issues encountered

## Implementation

### Progress Display Modes

1. **Interactive Mode** (TTY detected)
   - Use gum spinner for active operations
   - Show progress bar with repo count
   - Live update current phase/status
   - Color-coded status indicators

2. **Non-Interactive Mode** (--non-interactive or no TTY)
   - Print phase transitions as they happen
   - One line per significant event
   - Machine-parseable timestamps

3. **Verbose Mode** (--verbose)
   - Show agent output in real-time (streaming)
   - Print all ntm interactions
   - Detailed timing information

### Progress Components

```bash
# Example interactive display
[2/5] owner/repo-name
  └─ Phase 2: Generating commit plan... ⏳
  └─ Phase 1: Understanding ✓ (45s)
  └─ Preflight checks ✓

Progress: ████████░░░░░░░░ 40% (2/5 repos)
```

### Implementation Details

1. Use `gum spin` for spinners when available
2. Fall back to simple text updates without gum
3. Track timing per phase for summary
4. Buffer output to prevent interleaving with agent output
5. Support ANSI escape sequences for terminal control

## Stream Separation

Per AGENTS.md rules:
- All progress output goes to STDERR
- STDOUT reserved for JSON output in --json mode

## Related Beads

- Part of: bd-rhea (print_agent_sweep_summary)
- Parent epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Interactive mode shows spinner and progress bar
- [ ] Non-interactive mode prints clean status lines
- [ ] Verbose mode streams agent output
- [ ] Progress survives agent timeouts/errors gracefully
- [ ] Works with and without gum installed
- [ ] All output to stderr, stdout clean for --json","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:22:22.58904565-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T20:48:24.041355644-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T20:48:24.041355644-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented progress display with 10 functions for interactive/non-interactive modes, progress bar, ETA, and phase tracking. Integrated into run_sequential_agent_sweep().","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qhw3","depends_on_id":"bd-kczb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:36.128989169-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
